settings.q: Do lick mats and snuffle mats actually help with anxiety?
settings.a: They give dogs a repetitive, nose-led job to focus on. Licking and sniffing are natural self-soothing behaviours, so many owners use them around fireworks, storms and settling a new rescue. They're comfort and enrichment tools, not medical treatments.

settings.q: How much is delivery and how long does it take?
settings.a: Free UK delivery on orders over £35. Under that, the cost is shown in full at checkout — never added as a surprise. Most of our range is dispatched within 1–2 days and arrives in about 4–7 working days. The Car Boot Liner is stocked in our UK warehouse, so it dispatches same-day and arrives in 2–5 working days. We'd rather give you honest timings than promise next-day and miss it.

You have a 30-day money-back guarantee. You also have a legal 14-day cancellation right under the Consumer Contracts Regulations. For hygiene reasons, used lick mats, snuffle mats and grooming items can't be returned once opened unless faulty.

settings.q: Where do your products ship from?
settings.a: We're a small UK business run by one person, and we curate a tight range for anxious and rescue dogs rather than listing everything we can find. Some items are stocked here in the UK (like the Car Boot Liner); others are made and shipped by our manufacturing partner overseas, which is why those take about 4–7 working days — we tell you honestly rather than promise next-day. Either way, a real person answers your emails, usually the same day, and every order is covered by our 30-day guarantee.
